stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Iridium is a scarce and costly metal.
  • It is used as a catalyst in industrial chemical reactions.
  • Iridium catalysts typically require a substantial loading of iridium to perform well.
  • Researcher Priya Bhatnagar and her colleagues developed an iridium catalyst that disperses iridium across a graphene substrate.
  • Their catalyst was highly active and required only trace amounts of iridium (0.0005% of the atoms in the mixture).
stem: The student wants to explain an advantage of the new iridium catalyst developed by Priya Bhatnagar and her colleagues. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Researcher Priya Bhatnagar and her colleagues developed an iridium catalyst that disperses iridium, a scarce and costly metal, across a graphene substrate.
Iridium is a scarce and costly metal used as a catalyst in industrial chemical reactions; however, iridium catalysts typically require a substantial loading of the metal to perform well.
While still highly active, the new iridium catalyst requires far less of the scarce and costly metal than do other iridium catalysts.
Like other iridium catalysts, the new iridium catalyst requires a specific quantity of the metal to perform well.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It explains an advantage of the new catalyst by causally linking its high activity to a much smaller iridium requirement.

Choice A describes the new catalyst but doesn’t state an advantage. Choice B describes iridium catalysts generally without addressing the new catalyst’s advantage. Choice D states a similarity rather than an advantage.
